Category: Manufacturing: Chemicals, Food Manufacturing, Industrial Manufacturing, Textiles, Automotive, Pharmaceuticals, Electronics
Who is this for: Factory workers, production line operators, manufacturing technicians, quality control inspectors, maintenance staff, shift supervisors, plant managers, safety coordinators
Team or Dept this is for: Manufacturing, Operations, Health and Safety, Production, Quality Assurance, Maintenance, Plant Operations
Description: What happens when AI misinterprets movement or intent on the factory floor.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with factory floor operations or general manufacturing processes is helpful, though no prior knowledge of robotics or artificial intelligence is required to follow this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key risks that arise when AI-assisted robotic systems misinterpret human movement or intent on the factory floor, and explain how these misinterpretations can lead to safety incidents across manufacturing environments such as chemicals, food production, and automotive assembly. You will also be able to apply practical safety awareness strategies to recognise warning signs, respond appropriately to AI system errors, and support a safer working environment when operating alongside robotic technology.
